title: Clickhouse client -m doesn't submit query if you send semi-colon newline too quickly after the query
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Describe the unexpected behaviour
Ok, this is a bit of a weird setup, but I'm basically running clickhouse client in a terminal in my text editor (neovim). I have a command to send the contents of my currently open file (buffer) to the terminal. The use-case is that I have a SQL query in my text editor, and I'm basically trying to send it to clickhouse client to execute.
I'm running clickhouse client with the `-m` flag as my queries are multi-line, and without the flag, my queries would fail due to them being multi-line. My SQL file ends with a `;` and a newline.
For example
```
CREATE TABLE FOO
(
id String
)
ENGINE = MergeTree()
ORDER BY (id)
;
```
What I'm experiencing is that if I send the entire query to clickhouse-client at once, it doesn't get submitted. If I switch to my terminal and then type `;` and press enter, it submits.
The same issue happens if I manually copy & paste the query into clickhouse-client.
The crux of it seems to be that there needs to be a delay between sending the query and the `;` + newline.
If edit my editor to send the query, sleep 1 second, then send the `;` with a newline it gets submitted as I would normally expect. It seems that the main issue is there needs to be a delay of some sort before sending the `;`.
### Which ClickHouse versions are affected?
I'm using `25.9.2.1` but I've had this issue for a long time and only just now figured out the problem.
### How to reproduce
- 25.9.2.1
- `clickhouse client -m`
- Just copy and paste a multi-line query with a semi-colon into clickhouse client and notice it does not submit the query unless you type `;` and press enter yourself.
### Expected behavior
The query gets executed when copy/pasted.
### Error message and/or stacktrace
_No response_
### Additional context
_No response_
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table1 (id INTEGER, name VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMP);
CREATE TABLE test_table2 (id INTEGER, value DOUBLE, description VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO test_table1 VALUES (1, 'Alice', CURRENT_TIMESTAMP), (2, 'Bob', CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table2 VALUES (1, 3.14, 'Pi'), (2, NULL, 'Null Value');
SELECT name, value FROM test_table1 JOIN test_table2 ON test_table1.id = test_table2.id WHERE test_table2.value IS NOT NULL;
key_bug_features: Delay required after sending query and semicolon
